frontier_justification: Machine-learned interatomic potentials (MLIPs) are transforming molecular simulation and materials discovery in 2025-2026. Universal foundation models (MACE-MP-0, ORB, SevenNet) now cover the full periodic table and are actively used in drug/materials discovery pipelines.

papers.title: Generalized Neural-Network Representation of High-Dimensional Potential-Energy Surfaces
papers.key_contribution: Behler-Parrinello (BPNN): first neural network potential with physical symmetry (rotation/translation invariance) via atom-centered symmetry functions; enables MD at DFT accuracy.

idea_genome.problem_genome: Ab initio molecular dynamics (AIMD) is too slow for large systems; classical force fields lack accuracy; need fast and accurate interatomic potentials.
idea_genome.mechanism_genome: Decompose total energy as sum of atomic contributions; represent local environment via hand-crafted symmetry functions; fit with shallow neural network.
idea_genome.observation_genome: BPNN reproduces DFT potential energy surface for bulk silicon; enables ns-scale MD with quantum accuracy for ~100-atom systems.
idea_genome.limitation_genome: Hand-crafted symmetry functions require domain expertise; limited to learned element combinations; no long-range interactions; no message-passing between atoms.

papers.title: SchNet: A continuous-filter convolutional neural network for modeling quantum interactions
papers.key_contribution: SchNet: message-passing neural network with continuous-filter convolutions over pairwise distances; automatically learns atom interactions without hand-crafted features.

idea_genome.problem_genome: Behler-Parrinello requires hand-crafted symmetry functions; does not learn atom-atom interaction patterns automatically.
idea_genome.mechanism_genome: Continuous-filter convolutions: learnable radial basis functions applied to pairwise distances; message passing updates atomic representations iteratively.
idea_genome.observation_genome: SchNet achieves state-of-the-art on QM9 molecular property prediction; learns interaction patterns from data without feature engineering.
idea_genome.limitation_genome: Invariant to rotation/translation but not equivariant to them; only scalar (energy) predictions accurate; no vector/tensor properties; no angular information.

papers.title: Equivariant message passing for the prediction of tensorial properties and molecular spectra
papers.key_contribution: PaiNN: equivariant message passing using vector features alongside scalar features; enables prediction of tensorial molecular properties (dipole moments, polarizabilities).

idea_genome.problem_genome: SchNet produces only scalar (rotationally invariant) outputs; cannot predict tensorial properties like dipole moments that transform under rotations.
idea_genome.mechanism_genome: Equivariant architecture: augment scalar messages with equivariant vector features; vector features transform correctly under SO(3) rotations via cross-products.
idea_genome.observation_genome: PaiNN achieves state-of-the-art on MD17 (molecular forces) and QM9 tensorial properties; 6× faster than SE(3)-Transformer with same accuracy.
idea_genome.limitation_genome: Limited to first-order equivariance (vectors); higher-order tensors require more complex representations; single-element element encoding.

papers.title: MACE: Higher Order Equivariant Message Passing Neural Networks for Fast and Accurate Force Fields
papers.key_contribution: MACE: uses higher-order (l ≤ 3) equivariant message passing and many-body interactions; achieves 10-100× more data-efficient training than prior models.

idea_genome.problem_genome: PaiNN's first-order equivariance misses many-body angular interactions critical for bond angles, ring systems, and torsional barriers.
idea_genome.mechanism_genome: Higher-order E3-equivariant message passing via Clebsch-Gordan tensor products up to l=3; many-body messages aggregate neighbor interactions.
idea_genome.observation_genome: MACE achieves 10-100× lower force errors than SchNet/PaiNN with same data; state-of-the-art on MD17, ANI-1ccx; fast inference via sparse implementation.
idea_genome.limitation_genome: Trained per-material system; cannot transfer to unseen elements; each new system requires separate training dataset and model.

papers.title: A foundation model for atomistic materials chemistry
papers.key_contribution: MACE-MP-0: universal MLIP trained on Materials Project database covering all 89 elements; zero-shot transfer to molecular dynamics of diverse materials without retraining.

idea_genome.problem_genome: MACE and other MLIPs require training from scratch per material; cannot generalize to unseen chemical systems; need a universal force field.
idea_genome.mechanism_genome: Train MACE on Materials Project (>150K DFT calculations, all elements, diverse crystal structures); universal atomic representation; zero-shot transfer.
idea_genome.observation_genome: MACE-MP-0 achieves near-DFT accuracy on diverse materials (organic, inorganic, metals, surfaces) without any system-specific training.
idea_genome.limitation_genome: Training data biased toward equilibrium structures; poor at reaction pathways and rare events; no treatment of long-range electrostatics or dispersion.

papers.title: A Lightweight Universal Machine-Learning Interatomic Potential via Knowledge Distillation for Scalable Atomistic Simulations
papers.key_contribution: SevenNet-Nano: knowledge distillation from large universal NNPs (MACE-MP-0, SevenNet-Omni) to lightweight student models; achieves over an order-of-magnitude (>10×) inference speedup; enables universal force field at molecular dynamics production scale across diverse scenarios including plasma etching, ion diffusion, and liquid properties.

idea_genome.problem_genome: MACE-MP-0 achieves universal coverage but is computationally expensive for production-scale MD simulations with thousands of atoms; research-accuracy models are too slow for practical deployment.
idea_genome.mechanism_genome: Knowledge distillation: train compact student model to reproduce MACE-MP-0/SevenNet-Omni teacher model outputs; compact architecture maintains accuracy and transferability while drastically reducing inference cost.
idea_genome.observation_genome: SevenNet-Nano achieves high accuracy and strong transferability despite compact size; demonstrates >10× speedup over teacher models; handles diverse scenarios from standard material properties to extreme conditions.
idea_genome.limitation_genome: Student model inherits MACE-MP-0's limited treatment of long-range electrostatics; distillation compresses the model but does not fix the teacher's physical limitations around van der Waals and electrostatic interactions.

edges.evolution_focus: SchNet replaces BPNN's hand-crafted symmetry functions with learned continuous-filter message passing; shifts from feature engineering to end-to-end representation learning — a paradigm shift.
PaiNN extends SchNet's scalar message passing with equivariant vector features; same message-passing graph structure, adds geometric equivariance for tensorial outputs.
MACE extends PaiNN's first-order equivariance to higher-order (l≤3) via Clebsch-Gordan products; captures many-body interactions; same equivariant message-passing paradigm.
MACE architecture radiates from system-specific potentials to a universal foundation model covering all elements; same MACE architecture applied at scale to the full periodic table.

SevenNet-Nano radiates MACE-MP-0's universal coverage from research-accuracy scale to production MD scale via knowledge distillation; same universal potential paradigm adapted to the niche of large-scale practical simulation.

open_question: SevenNet-Nano achieves inference speedup via distillation but the student model inherits MACE-MP-0's limited treatment of long-range electrostatics. What mechanism would enable a lightweight universal potential to capture long-range electrostatic and van der Waals effects with calibrated accuracy on charged, polar, defective, and heterogeneous systems without sacrificing the >10× speedup over teacher models?

question.name: Question
question.description: Present only the domain/subfield and the curated May-2026 research question; ask the model to propose a next-step research direction in Computational Physics / Materials Science.

library.name: Library
library.description: Present the curated research frontier plus 6 trace papers in random order with paper titles and brief abstracts, but without explicit lineage order, genome fields, or dynamics labels; ask the model to propose the next research direction.

lineage.name: Lineage
lineage.description: Present the curated research frontier plus the ordered trace with genome metadata, GenomeDiff-style change evidence, and parent-child relationships; ask the model to predict the next evolutionary step without naming predefined category labels.

frontier_evidence.title: A Lightweight Universal Machine-Learning Interatomic Potential via Knowledge Distillation for Scalable Atomistic Simulations
frontier_evidence.unresolved_gap: Student model inherits MACE-MP-0's limited treatment of long-range electrostatics; distillation compresses the model but does not fix the teacher's physical limitations around van der Waals and electrostatic interactions.
frontier_evidence.why_frontier: Used as the May 2026 frontier anchor: the arena question targets a limitation that remains unresolved at the endpoint of this lineage.

frontier_status.manual_review: Re-audited on 2026-05-05 for appendix release: active arena trace has a 2025/2026 frontier endpoint, a non-solved open research question, valid forward lineage edges, and generation settings aligned with post-hoc dynamics evaluation.
frontier_status.prompt_role: The same research question is shown in Question, Library, and Lineage settings; only the supporting literature/lineage context changes.
